sprint: Required Tap-to-Begin Gate — Closes the Real Autoplay Gap the Preload Gate Left Open
task: Root-caused a real, still-reproducing report ('i cleared cached and reloaded the page the ambience and lumo voice did not came even for traveller') even after the prior sprint's spinner-style preload gate shipped: that gate correctly primes/preloads audio and waits for readiness, but never requires or waits for an actual user gesture before proceeding -- browser autoplay policy silently blocks every un-muted audio.play() call with no real click/tap in the chain, so a Traveller who hadn't yet clicked anything by the time the Gateway auto-played through got total silence, exactly as reported. Fixed with a new required 'Tap to Begin' gate (js/gatewaySequence.js's showBeginGate()) shown as the very first thing inside the Gateway's own begin() sequence -- its tap synchronously calls AudioManager.init()+playFoundation(), genuinely satisfying the browser's gesture requirement, before the rest of the cinematic (session detection, Gate video preload, greeting) proceeds. A second, real bug was found and fixed while building this, via a live DOM MutationObserver trace rather than assumed: calling the resume callback synchronously inside the gate's own click handler let the still-bubbling tap event immediately re-trigger a whole-overlay skip-click listener attached moments earlier, collapsing the entire multi-second sequence to one tick -- fixed by deferring the resume via setTimeout(fn,0), letting the tap event finish bubbling before anything downstream can react to a new click listener. Verified via Playwright (with prefers-reduced-motion explicitly disabled, since this sandbox's headless Chromium reports it true by default): the gate shows, a tap fires all 5 real Foundation-layer play() calls, and the Gateway genuinely continues playing afterward (Gate video still mounted 1.2s later) rather than collapsing instantly; zero console errors.
